source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure route table changes are monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure route table changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. Routing tables are used to route network traffic between subnets and to network gateways. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes to route tables.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to route tables will help ensure that all VPC traffic flows through the expected path and prevent any accidental or intentional modifications that may lead to uncontrolled network traffic. An alarm should be triggered every time an AWS API call is performed to create, replace, delete, or disassociate a route table.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{($.eventSource = ec2.amazonaws.com) && ($.eventName = CreateRoute) || ($.eventName = CreateRouteTable) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRoute) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRouteTableAssociation) || ($.eventName = DeleteRouteTable) || ($.eventName = DeleteRoute) || ($.eventName = DisassociateRouteTable) }" 4. Note the <route-table-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <route-table-changes- metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<route-table-changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for route table changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> -- filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = CreateRoute) || ($.eventName = CreateRouteTable) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRoute) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRouteTableAssociation) || ($.eventName = DeleteRouteTable) || ($.eventName = DeleteRoute) || ($.eventName = DisassociateRouteTable) }' Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <route-table-changes- alarm> --metric-name <route-table-changes-metric> --statistic Sum -- period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn> References: 1. CCE-79198-8 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79198-8 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be effectively audited through AWS APIs, using boto3 SDK and AWS CLI commands. It involves checking configurations related to CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, metric filters, SNS topics, and CloudWatch alarms, all of which are accessible and verifiable through AWS APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudwatch_route_table_changes_monitoring_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

1. **AWS API Calls and Boto3 SDK Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to list all CloudTrail trails and find those with `IsMultiRegionTrail` set to `True`.
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=trail_name)` to ensure logging is enabled (`IsLogging` is `True`) for each trail.
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors(TrailName=trail_name)` to check that the event selectors include management events with `ReadWriteType` set to `All`.

2. **CloudWatch Logs and Metric Filters:**
   - Use `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=trail_log_group_name)` to retrieve all metric filters and ensure there is one with the filter pattern that captures route table changes.

3. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<route-table-changes-metric>]'` to ensure CloudWatch alarms are configured to trigger on the route table changes metric.
   - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>` to verify at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** 
     - The CloudTrail is multi-region, active, and configured to capture management events.
     - The appropriate metric filter exists on the CloudWatch log group.
     - A CloudWatch alarm is configured to trigger on changes to route tables.
     - There is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic linked to the alarm.
   - **Fail:** 
     - Missing any of the above configurations or having inactive components (e.g., inactive alarm or no CloudTrail logging).

By utilizing the combination of AWS CLI commands and boto3 for API interactions, this control ensures that route table changes are being effectively monitored and that alerts are set up correctly to notify about any such changes. This approach aligns with industry-standard practices for automating compliance audits.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively audited using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI, which aligns with the capabilities of these tools to interact with AWS services programmatically.
- The use of AWS SDK (boto3) and AWS CLI commands is appropriate for checking configurations related to CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, metric filters, SNS topics, and CloudWatch alarms.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The boto3 methods and AWS CLI commands used are valid for the tasks described.
- There is a minor oversight in ensuring that the metric filter pattern specifically captures route table changes. The filter pattern should be explicitly defined to match route table change events.
- The program name `aws_cloudwatch_route_table_changes_monitoring_configured` is descriptive but could be more specific regarding the security aspect being checked.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Metric Filter Pattern:** Ensure that the metric filter pattern is explicitly defined to capture route table changes. This could involve specifying the exact API calls related to route table modifications, such as `CreateRoute`, `DeleteRoute`, `ReplaceRoute`, etc.
- **Program Name:** The program name could be improved for clarity and specificity. A suggestion could be `aws_cloudwatch_route_table_change_detection_configured` to emphasize the detection aspect.
- **Additional Checks:** Consider verifying that the SNS topic used for notifications is configured with appropriate permissions to allow CloudWatch to publish messages.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and mostly accurate. The use of AWS APIs and services is correctly aligned with the control requirements. The minor improvements suggested do not significantly affect the overall confidence in the assessment.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Implement the suggested improvements, particularly ensuring the metric filter pattern is correctly defined to capture route table changes and refining the program name for clarity.
- The overall approach and use of automation are appropriate, so no reclassification is necessary.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that all required audit checks—verifying CloudTrail multi-region trails, CloudWatch Logs integration, metric filters for route table changes, and CloudWatch alarms—are fully accessible via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). The specific API calls and filter patterns for detecting route table changes are well-documented and can be programmatically validated. No manual inspection or console-only steps are required.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_cloudwatch_route_table_change_detection_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail Trails, CloudWatch Log Groups, CloudWatch Metric Filters, CloudWatch Alarms, SNS Topics (optional for notifications)
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`  
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=...)`  
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=...)`  
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`  
  - `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=...)` (if alarm notifications are required)
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - At least one CloudTrail trail with `IsMultiRegionTrail=True` and `IsLogging=True`
  - The trail is configured to send logs to a CloudWatch Log Group
  - The Log Group has a metric filter with a filter pattern matching route table change events:  
    `{ ($.eventName = CreateRoute) || ($.eventName = CreateRouteTable) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRoute) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRouteTableAssociation) || ($.eventName = DeleteRoute) || ($.eventName = DeleteRouteTable) }`
  - A CloudWatch Alarm exists for the metric generated by this filter and is in an enabled state
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - All above properties are present and correctly configured
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - Missing multi-region CloudTrail trail, missing/inactive logging, missing or incorrect metric filter, missing or disabled alarm, or metric filter pattern does not match required route table change events

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The only minor difference was in the specificity of the program name and the explicitness of the metric filter pattern. I resolved this by adopting the more precise program name and ensuring the metric filter pattern is explicitly defined as per CIS guidance.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
